title: 21:40 ГўВҖВ” December 9, 2021
body_text:
21:40 ГўВҖВ” December 9, 2021
Updates to Federal Warrants and BC Provincial Cheques Reimbursement Claims
Payments Canada revised CPA Rule G8, Procedures/Criteria Pertaining to the Encashment of Receiver General Warrants for Non-Customers. Effective immediately,
counterfeit
Receiver General Warrants will be indemnified if the financial institution has made attempts to recover the funds before seeking reimbursement (maximum $1500).
For BC Employment and Assistance cheques, the required documentation for claims has been revised. The following items must be included with the claim submitted for reimbursement:
ГўВҖВў
The original cheque, a certified true copy, or a bank-issued Return Replacement Document (RRD) endorsed with the customerГўВҖВҷs signature
ГўВҖВў
A letter requesting reimbursement, including but not limited to
ГўВҲВ’
Details of any funds that have already been recovered against the wrongful cashing of the BC Employment and Assistance cheque
ГўВҲВ’
Details of the sequence of events and the diligence followed in the cashing of the BC Employment and Assistance cheque
ГўВҲВ’
Confirmation that the cheque was endorsed by the individual believed to be the customer in front of an employee of your credit union
ГўВҖВў
Proof of two pieces of legitimate ID consistent with the MinistryГўВҖВҷs requirements taken to identify the customer at the time of cashing and referenced on the back of the cheque
ГўВҖВў
Proof that the signature from the ID taken matches the signature endorsement on the back of the cheque
Clearing and Deposits Volume
Deposit Processing Part
Section 5.5, Government of Canada Warrants
Updated content to reflect that credit unions will be indemnified for counterfeit Government of Canada warrants under Rule G8. However, this Rule still doesnГўВҖВҷt indemnify credit unions against warrants with material alterations.
Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part
Section 3.1, CPA Rules
Added a description for Rule G8, Procedures/Criteria Pertaining to the Encashment of Receiver General Warrants for Non-Customers.
Section 7.1, Requesting Reimbursement for Fraudulent or Non-Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
Added details of the new requirements for documents submitted with reimbursement claim.
Section 7.3, Requesting Reimbursement for Dishonoured Government of Canada Warrants
Updated content to reflect that credit unions
will be indemnified for
counterfeit
Government of Canada warrants under Rule G8. However, this Rule still doesnГўВҖВҷt indemnify credit unions against cheques with material alterations.
